mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) across 100+ historical rate cuts, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer spending and corporate earnings deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or the signal's Sharpe ratio turns negative in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates supply chain bottlenecks and output gaps, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during recovery phases consistently coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns, or the mechanism yields a negative information coefficient across all recovery periods since 2000.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25 percent, extreme monetary accommodation compresses equity risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are negative in more than 55% of observations where FEDFUNDS drops below 0.25%, or the threshold model fails out-of-sample accuracy tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and falling total nonfarm payrolls signals structural labor market deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive 60-day returns in over 60% of historical cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant when controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real gross domestic product reflect sustained economic expansion that directly supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth consistently precedes negative 5-day S&P 500 returns, or the signal's predictive power vanishes (Rank IC < 0.01) in out-of-sample walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production expanding above its 2017 baseline during a late-cycle expansion phase confirms capacity utilization strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns turn negative when INDPRO exceeds 100 during late-cycle phases, or the threshold condition fails to improve classification accuracy over a naive baseline.
